_note: Egor article_drill batch 1 — 15 items at B2, weighted toward his documented 'articles 50% (n=8)' weak pattern from coach_notes. IELTS Academic + B2 business register. Pattern distribution: 4 a_the_swap (second mention / inherited specificity), 5 zero_uncountable (research, information, advice, knowledge, mathematics — IELTS-critical), 3 generic_vs_specific (definite unique concept / generic plural), 2 fixed_phrase (on the basis of, at the heart of), 1 country_with_the (the UK). Themes: KPMG audit/consulting, Almaty, mathematics, UK master's, research papers. Single-blank chip UX (a/an/the/—). EN coach language. Push to exercises_library/article_drill/{id} via tools/push_library.js.

exercises.sentence_template: The team finalised an audit report yesterday. {1} report was sent to the client this morning.

blanks.reasoning: Second mention. The report was introduced with 'an audit report' — once it's identified, switch to 'the'. Russian 'отчёт' carries no article cue, so this swap is the most common B2 calque slip.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Second-mention rule: introduce with 'a/an', refer back with 'the'.

exercises.sentence_template: My supervisor said I need to do {1} more research before submitting my master's application.

blanks.reasoning: 'Research' is always uncountable in academic English — no 'a/an', no plural 'researches'. Russian 'исследование' is countable; this calque is the top RU slip in IELTS Writing Task 2.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Research / information / evidence / advice / knowledge are all uncountable — never 'a research'.

exercises.sentence_template: The auditor asked for {1} information about the company's revenue from last quarter.

blanks.reasoning: 'Information' is always uncountable — no 'a/an', no plural. Russian 'информация' patterns differently; 'an information' / 'informations' are top RU calques in business English.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Information is uncountable — use 'some information' or 'a piece of information' if a unit is needed.

exercises.sentence_template: On {1} basis of these findings, the partner decided to expand the audit scope.

blanks.reasoning: 'On the basis of' — fixed prepositional phrase, always 'the'. Same family: 'in the light of', 'in the case of'. Compare: 'in line with' (no article).

exercises.fallback_feedback: Memorise as a chunk: on the basis of, in the light of, in the case of.

exercises.sentence_template: After two years at KPMG, Egor decided to apply to {1} university in the UK.

blanks.reasoning: First mention, indefinite — one of many possible UK universities. Compare: 'apply to the university' = the specific one we already discussed.

exercises.fallback_feedback: First mention of a non-unique noun → 'a/an'.

exercises.sentence_template: He's planning to do his master's in {1} UK, probably at LSE or Imperial.

blanks.reasoning: Country names containing 'kingdom', 'states', 'republic', or that are plural ('the Netherlands', 'the Philippines') take 'the'. Compare: 'Kazakhstan' (no article), 'Britain' (no article).

exercises.fallback_feedback: The UK / The USA / The UAE / The Netherlands — fixed list to memorise.

exercises.sentence_template: {1} mathematicians often work alone, but consulting requires constant collaboration.

blanks.reasoning: Generic plural — talking about mathematicians as a class. ✗ 'The mathematicians' would mean a specific group. Russian plural carries no article cue, so the gap defaults correctly to no article here.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Generic plural class statement → no article. 'The X' would specify a particular group.

exercises.sentence_template: Last week I read a paper on stochastic models. {1} paper completely changed how I think about risk.

blanks.reasoning: Second mention. Paper introduced with 'a paper'; reference back uses 'the'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Sentence-1 'a' / sentence-2 'the' is the canonical second-mention pattern.

exercises.sentence_template: My professor gave me {1} excellent advice about which UK programmes to target.

blanks.reasoning: 'Advice' is uncountable — even with an adjective. ✗ 'an excellent advice' is the top B2 RU calque. Use 'a piece of advice' if a singular unit is needed.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Advice is uncountable. 'A piece of advice' for a unit; otherwise no article.

exercises.sentence_template: In game theory, {1} prisoner's dilemma is the most-studied example of strategic interaction.

blanks.reasoning: 'The prisoner's dilemma' — a named, unique concept. Use 'the' for definite named ideas: the central limit theorem, the Pythagorean theorem, the Black-Scholes model.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Named theoretical concepts take 'the' — the X theorem / the Y dilemma / the Z paradox.

exercises.sentence_template: After his master's he wants to work at {1} university, maybe even teach part-time.

blanks.reasoning: First mention, indefinite — any university. Compare: 'work at the university' = the specific one previously named.

exercises.fallback_feedback: First mention of a non-unique entity → indefinite article.

exercises.sentence_template: Almaty is at {1} heart of Central Asia's tech startup scene.

blanks.reasoning: 'At the heart of' — fixed phrase. Memorise as a chunk: at the heart of, at the centre of, at the core of.

exercises.fallback_feedback: At the heart of / at the centre of — fixed phrases, always 'the'.

exercises.sentence_template: {1} mathematics has always been Egor's strongest subject.

blanks.reasoning: Academic subject names take no article — mathematics, physics, history, economics. ✗ 'The mathematics' would mean specific maths (e.g. of a particular paper).

exercises.fallback_feedback: Subject names (maths, physics, history) → no article.

exercises.sentence_template: KPMG won a major audit contract last month. {1} contract is worth over five million dollars in fees.

blanks.reasoning: Second mention — contract introduced with 'a major audit contract', now identified.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Once introduced, the same noun in the next sentence takes 'the'.

exercises.sentence_template: For consulting, {1} knowledge of Excel is more important than knowledge of advanced maths.

blanks.reasoning: 'Knowledge' is uncountable. Same family as information / evidence / research / advice. ✗ 'a knowledge' is a top B2 RU calque.
